prompt: We've got a mainframe from 1997 that handles payroll through green-screen terminals. The team wants to interact with it through Slack. Make that happen without rebuilding the payroll logic.
My fish tank has pH, temperature, and salinity sensors wired to an Arduino. I want to be able to ask 'how are the fish doing?' and get a plain-English answer.
The city parking meters report occupancy via MQTT but there's no user-facing interface. Design something that lets parking enforcement ask natural questions about open spots in a zone.
Our test lab has 40 devices you can only configure through individual SSH sessions. Can you create a unified conversational front so QA can say 'reboot device cluster B' in one command?
I want to uplift the interface on our ancient FTP-based file exchange system. Users should be able to say 'send the Q3 report to accounting' without knowing FTP commands.
We have a PLC controlling our manufacturing line. Nobody can read the HMI screens. Wrap the PLC's Modbus registers in something a floor supervisor can talk to.
The irrigation system on our farm has a perfectly good API that nobody uses because the docs are incomprehensible. Give it a voice so the farmhands can just say what zone to water.

Our DNS infrastructure is managed entirely through dig and nsupdate. Build an agent layer so junior ops can manage records by describing what they want in English, with guardrails so they can't nuke production zones.
